channel.title: Daily AI Digest
channel.description: Curated AI/ML signals — zero LLMs, pure algorithmic curation

item.title: Let's Buy Spirit Air
item.description: 💬 Major HN discussion (314 comments)

item.description: 💬 Major HN discussion (165 comments)

item.description: 💬 Active HN discussion (50 comments)

item.title: The 'Hidden' Costs of Great Abstractions
item.description: 💬 Active HN discussion (61 comments)

item.title: Online Self-Calibration Against Hallucination in Vision-Language Models
item.description: 📄 Featured on Hugging Face Daily Papers — Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) often suffer from hallucinations, generating descriptions that include visual details absent from the input image. Recent preference alignment methods typically rely on supervision distilled from stronger models such as GPT. However, this offline paradigm intr...

item.title: End-to-End Autoregressive Image Generation with 1D Semantic Tokenizer
item.description: 📄 Featured on Hugging Face Daily Papers — Autoregressive image modeling relies on visual tokenizers to compress images into compact latent representations. We design an end-to-end training pipeline that jointly optimizes reconstruction and generation, enabling direct supervision from generation results to the tokenizer. This contrasts wi...

item.title: From Skill Text to Skill Structure: The Scheduling-Structural-Logical Representation for Agent Skills
item.description: 📄 Featured on Hugging Face Daily Papers — LLM agents increasingly rely on reusable skills, capability packages that combine instructions, control flow, constraints, and tool calls. In most current agent systems, however, skills are still represented by text-heavy artifacts, including SKILL{.}md-style documents and structured records whos...

item.title: Let ViT Speak: Generative Language-Image Pre-training
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — In this paper, we present \textbf{Gen}erative \textbf{L}anguage-\textbf{I}mage \textbf{P}re-training (GenLIP), a minimalist generative pretraining framework for Vision Transformers (ViTs) designed for multimodal large language models (MLLMs). To better align vision encoders with the autoregressiv...

item.title: UniVidX: A Unified Multimodal Framework for Versatile Video Generation via Diffusion Priors
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Recent progress has shown that video diffusion models (VDMs) can be repurposed for diverse multimodal graphics tasks. However, existing methods often train separate models for each problem setting, which fixes the input-output mapping and limits the modeling of correlations across modalities. We ...

item.title: Map2World: Segment Map Conditioned Text to 3D World Generation
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — 3D world generation is essential for applications such as immersive content creation or autonomous driving simulation. Recent advances in 3D world generation have shown promising results; however, these methods are constrained by grid layouts and suffer from inconsistencies in object scale throug...

item.title: LASE: Language-Adversarial Speaker Encoding for Indic Cross-Script Identity Preservation
item.description: 📄 New in cs.SD, cs.CL — A speaker encoder used in multilingual voice cloning should treat the same speaker identically regardless of which script the audio was uttered in. Off-the-shelf encoders do not, and the failure is accent-conditional. On a 1043-pair Western-accented voice corpus across English, Hindi, Telugu, and...

item.title: Themis: Training Robust Multilingual Code Reward Models for Flexible Multi-Criteria Scoring
item.description: 📄 New in cs.SE, cs.LG — Reward models (RMs) have become an indispensable fixture of the language model (LM) post-training playbook, enabling policy alignment and test-time scaling. Research on the application of RMs in code generation, however, has been comparatively sparse, with existing work largely focusing on execut...

item.title: Learning to Act and Cooperate for Distributed Black-Box Consensus Optimization
item.description: 📄 New in cs.MA, cs.NE — Distributed blackbox consensus optimization is a fundamental problem in multi-agent systems, where agents must improve a global objective using only local objective queries and limited neighbor communication. Existing methods largely rely on handcrafted update rules and static cooperation pattern...

item.title: Learning while Deploying: Fleet-Scale Reinforcement Learning for Generalist Robot Policies
item.description: 📄 New in cs.RO — Generalist robot policies increasingly benefit from large-scale pretraining, but offline data alone is insufficient for robust real-world deployment. Deployed robots encounter distribution shifts, long-tail failures, task variations, and human correction opportunities that fixed demonstration dat...

item.title: LearningCircuit / local-deep-research
item.description: 💬 Active HN discussion (34 comments) · ⭐ 143 GitHub stars today — Local Deep Research achieves ~95% on SimpleQA benchmark (tested with Qwen 3.6). Supports local and cloud LLMs (Ollama, Google, Anthropic, ...). Searches 10+ sources - arXiv, PubMed, web, and your private documents. Everything Local & Encrypted.

item.title: HandsOnLLM / Hands-On-Large-Language-Models
item.description: ⭐ 82 stars today on GitHub · ⭐ 25.7k total stars — Official code repo for the O'Reilly Book - "Hands-On Large Language Models"

item.title: TauricResearch / TradingAgents
item.description: ⭐ 3.3k GitHub stars today · ⭐ 66.0k total stars — TradingAgents: Multi-Agents LLM Financial Trading Framework

item.title: 6 AI Agents That Replaced My Entire Workflow in 2026
item.description: 🆕 New article — <div class="medium-feed-item"><p class="medium-feed-image"><a href="https://medium.com/@hii_mohit/6-ai-agents-that-replaced-my-entire-workflow-in-2026-67a02d3ad024?source=rss------ai_agent-5"><img src="https://cdn-images-1.medium.com/max/1400/0*so-KgJzQjR_GDrP0.png" width="1400" /></a></p><p clas...

item.title: cocoindex-io / cocoindex
item.description: ⭐ 163 GitHub stars today — Incremental engine for long horizon agents 🌟 Star if you like it!

item.title: Q00 / ouroboros
item.description: ⭐ 102 GitHub stars today — Agent OS: Stop prompting. Start specifying.
